meta.title: AllyOS Wellness IV/IM — Universal Hard-Stop Gate Package (peptide-line shared)
meta.curated_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD (FL ME 84789) — RenuviaMD® PLLC, AllyOS Compliance Division
meta.purpose: The single shared RED-level hard-stop layer that every peptide stack family (FAM01–FAM09) routes to BEFORE any optional IV/IM wellness support is matched. It introduces NO new ingredient ceilings or screens — those live only in the locked iv-module. It (a) delegates the universal acute hold/referral to the locked iv-module, (b) states the wellness-elective principle, and (c) consolidates the peptide-context absolute contraindications by family so the deterministic engine has one place to route the family gate_package string.

delegates_to.universal_hold: gates.vital_hold_referral_criteria (IVIM_GATE_VITALS) — AUTHORITATIVE; do NOT restate or relax

meta.one_safety_source_principle: This package NEVER overrides or relaxes a locked gate. If ANY delegated gate says HOLD, this package says HOLD. It can only ADD a stop, never remove one. Where it lists a criterion that also appears in IVIM_GATE_VITALS, the locked iv-module text is authoritative and this listing is a non-authoritative pointer.
meta.no_doses: This package issues no doses, rates, or volumes. On PASS it routes to the specific locked standing order, which enforces ceilings and ingredient screens.
meta.governance_posture: Inherits the locked iv-module posture: AllyOS is educational / advisory. This package SURFACES a strong HOLD / provider-review advisory when a red-flag criterion is present — it tells the provider WHAT is required and WHY — but it does NOT itself stop, block, or enforce. The provider reviews and signs; they own every decision and the responsibility flows to the NPI owner / clinic. The ONLY exception is a practice where RenuviaMD is the Medical Director of record ('MD-of-record' mode), where gate enforcement applies because RenuviaMD then carries the supervising authority and liability. Default is advisory-only (never blocks). The terms 'hard stop' / 'DO NOT infuse' below are CLINICAL advisories (the recommended determination), not engine enforcement.
meta.claim_discipline_ref: Per-family do_not_claim and evidence_language travel with each family in peptide-stack-families.json and peptide-iv-support.json; this package does not restate them. FAM09 carries the AOD-9604 efficacy-limitation inline because it is load-bearing for that family's safety framing.

wellness_elective_principle.rule: Optional IV/IM support for a peptide patient is WELLNESS-ELECTIVE. An acute symptom is never an indication to infuse — it is a reason to STOP. If a red-flag symptom or unstable finding is present, this is not a wellness visit: HOLD and refer to the appropriate level of care. Wellness IV/IM is matched only after the patient is clinically stable and all gates clear.
wellness_elective_principle.no_masking: Never use an elective wellness IV/IM to treat, mask, or substitute for evaluation of an acute symptom — e.g., do not 'hydrate away' persistent vomiting/AKI, do not 'energy IV' away chest pain or hypoglycemia, do not 'immune IV' a febrile/septic patient.
wellness_elective_principle.selective_not_mandatory: No peptide stack auto-indicates an IV. Support is matched only on a wellness goal or a patient-factor trigger AND a clear gate pass. Peptide use alone is never a trigger.

tier_red_universal_hard_stop.applies_to: ALL families (FAM01–FAM09), every encounter, before any support match.
tier_red_universal_hard_stop.source: DELEGATED to the locked iv-module IVIM_GATE_VITALS. Reproduced below as a non-authoritative pointer only — the locked text governs.
tier_red_universal_hard_stop.action: DO NOT infuse any elective wellness IV/IM. HOLD and refer (PCP / urgent care / ED / EMS-911 as indicated). Notify the Medical Director for QA AFTER appropriate clinical action has been taken.
tier_red_universal_hard_stop.delegated_criteria_ref: iv-module gates.vital_hold_referral_criteria.criteria (AUTHORITATIVE — the locked thresholds live there; this package does not restate the numbers to avoid drift)
pointer_categories: Abnormal vital signs (BP / HR / temperature / SpO2) per IVIM_GATE_VITALS
Acute chest pain · acute neurologic symptoms · altered mental status
Moderate/severe dehydration · persistent vomiting/diarrhea · syncope · fever · acute infection
CHF or fluid restriction · CKD/dialysis/unknown renal status in a high-risk patient
Known arrhythmia · heart block · unstable cardiac disease
Active malignancy or active oncology treatment without written oncologist clearance

tier_red_universal_absolute_contraindica.applies_to: ALL families — independent of the chosen support ingredient.
tier_red_universal_absolute_contraindica.rule: Adults ≥18 only. Pregnancy / suspected pregnancy / lactation is HOLD or provider-review for every elective wellness IV/IM (never auto-clear). Active malignancy / active oncology treatment without written oncologist clearance is a hard stop (delegated to IVIM_GATE_VITALS). These are peptide-line restatements of the locked universal gate, not new rules.

red_hard_stops: Personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 — GLP/GIP backbone boxed warning (peptide-side; gates the stack, not the IV ingredient)
Acute or recent pancreatitis; severe persistent abdominal pain radiating to back → DO NOT infuse, urgent evaluation (ref GLP_GATE_002)
Gallbladder red flags: RUQ pain with fever · jaundice · dark urine / pale stools → urgent evaluation (ref GLP_GATE_002)
Severe GI fluid loss with orthostasis/syncope/AKI symptoms or known CKD with GI symptoms → not wellness; renal/electrolyte assessment or escalation (ref GLP_GATE_001)
Hypoglycemia symptoms / low glucose on insulin or sulfonylurea → glucose context required; do NOT mask hypoglycemia as fatigue (ref GLP_GATE_003)
Active malignancy = hard stop (GH/IGF-1 axis is mitogenic) — peptide-side stack gate
Pregnancy / lactation
Active cardiac instability / unstable arrhythmia → delegate IVIM_GATE_VITALS
Active chest pain / unstable arrhythmia / unstable cardiovascular symptoms → HOLD, urgent evaluation
Active malignancy review (NAD/redox pathway) before infusing
Advanced CKD/ESRD or advanced liver disease → provider review
Pregnancy / lactation
review_not_stop: NAD+/NR high-risk gate (CV symptoms, slow-infusion tolerability)
CoQ10 is IM-only — no oil-based IV route (ING_COQ10)
family_absolute_contraindications.niagen_note: Clinical use of injectable Niagen/NR requires the clinic's MEDICAL DIRECTOR to sign a standing order; verify current FDA 503B status. NAD+ precursor, distinct from NAD+ IV.

red_hard_stops: Active OR prior malignancy / current cancer surveillance = hard stop — BPC-157, TB-500 and GHK-Cu are pro-angiogenic (peptide-side stack gate)
GHK-Cu: Wilson disease / copper-overload → hard stop; review cumulative copper
Pregnancy / lactation; age <18
Acute injury or infection red flags needing evaluation → refer first, not infuse
review_not_stop: renal/hepatic amino-acid gate before any amino support (ING_AMINOBLEND)
vitamin C G6PD / renal gate if VitC support matched (ING_VITC)

red_hard_stops: Active OR prior malignancy = hard stop — GHK-Cu / BPC-157 / TB-500 pro-angiogenic (peptide-side stack gate)
GHK-Cu: Wilson disease / copper-overload → hard stop
Pregnancy / lactation
review_not_stop: biotin lab interference (troponin/TSH/hCG) — hold before labs / inform lab (ING_BIOTIN)

red_hard_stops: Uncontrolled hypertension or known cardiovascular disease = label contraindication for PT-141/bremelanotide axis → hard stop
Abnormal BP/HR · chest pain · syncope at the visit → HOLD (delegate IVIM_GATE_VITALS)
Hormone-sensitive malignancy (breast / prostate / endometrial / ovarian) with HPG-axis agents → hard stop
Known or suspected pituitary tumor / adenoma → hard stop for the kisspeptin-10 / gonadorelin HPG-stimulation axis (GnRH-axis stimulation risk)
Pregnancy / lactation
family_absolute_contraindications.delegated_red_flags_ref: peptide-stack-families.json FAM06.key_gate (authoritative — full hormone-sensitive-malignancy + pituitary-tumor screen); safety-gate-map FAM06.primary_red_flags covers the CV/BP/HR subset only

red_hard_stops: Acute neurologic symptoms (focal deficit, severe/thunderclap headache, new confusion) → DO NOT infuse, urgent evaluation
Active suicidality / mania / acute psychiatric instability → refer, do not infuse
Pregnancy / lactation
Active infection / fever / hypoxia / confusion / sepsis symptoms = DO NOT infuse — medical evaluation
Active malignancy review before thymic/immunomodulatory peptides
Pregnancy / lactation
Active malignancy (GH/IGF-1 agents in this family) = hard stop
Pregnancy / lactation
family_absolute_contraindications.claim_discipline: AOD-9604 efficacy-limitation language is REQUIRED (failed a 24-week, 536-subject weight-loss trial). This is claim discipline, not a safety stop.

routing.principle: Each family's gate_package string is a composition: 'wellness_iv_hard_stop' resolves to THIS package; the other tokens resolve to their own packages (glp_support gates → peptide-iv-support glp_context_safety_gates; patient_factor_modifier_rules → peptide module; ingredient_axis_match → peptide-iv-support ingredient_matrix; niagen_nr_standing_order → ING_NIAGEN_NR + protocol WEL-16, now LOCKED in the iv-module v1.1 (effective 2026-06-29) — clinical use still requires the clinic's Medical Director to sign their own standing order and verify FDA 503B status).
evaluation_order: 1. WIHS_RED_UNIVERSAL (delegated IVIM_GATE_VITALS) — acute instability / universal holds
4. composed package tokens (GLP drug-context gates, patient-factor modifiers, ingredient axis match)
5. ingredient_specific_screens (delegated to iv-module) for whatever ingredient is matched
6. PASS → route to the specific locked standing order (doses/ceilings enforced there)
routing.any_hold_wins: If any layer returns HOLD/STOP, the encounter is HOLD/refer. No layer can clear a stop set by a lower-numbered layer.

validation_cases: FAM01 GLP patient with RUQ pain + fever asks for a 'recovery drip' → RED hard stop (gallbladder), do not infuse, urgent evaluation.
FAM01 GLP patient on insulin, shaky, glucose 58 → RED, do not 'energy IV'; treat/eval hypoglycemia.
FAM02 GH-recovery patient with active breast cancer wants amino recovery IV → RED hard stop (mitogenic axis).
FAM03 mito patient with active chest pain wants NAD+ → RED, HOLD, urgent evaluation.
FAM04 repair patient with prior melanoma wants BPC/TB-500 + VitC glow IV → RED hard stop (pro-angiogenic + prior malignancy).
FAM05 glow patient, Wilson disease, wants GHK-Cu support → RED hard stop (copper overload).
FAM06 sexual-vitality patient with uncontrolled HTN → RED hard stop (PT-141 axis label contraindication).
FAM07 calm/sleep patient with new focal weakness → RED, do not infuse, urgent neuro evaluation.
FAM08 patient febrile/septic-appearing asks immune IV → RED, do not infuse, medical evaluation.
FAM09 patient wants AOD 'fat-burner' IV → allowed only with efficacy-limitation language + GH/IGF-1 and hepatic/gallbladder review; pregnancy → HOLD.
Any family, pregnant patient, fully stable, wants a beauty drip → HOLD/provider-review (never auto-clear).
Any family, all gates clear, stable adult, clear wellness goal → PASS → route to the specific locked standing order.

signoff_ledger.status: LOCKED 2026-06-29 — 3-auditor pass with all load-bearing notes applied; MD sign-off recorded.

md_signoff.role: Medical Director / curator — editorial governance sign-off (frameworks, once). This is NOT a per-patient clinical signature; the treating provider owns and signs each encounter determination in their own record.
md_signoff.attests: This shared hard-stop gate package delegates to the locked iv-module (IVIM_GATE_VITALS) without relaxing any locked gate, consolidates the 9-family absolute contraindications faithfully to peptide-stack-families.json, and is advisory by default (enforcement only in MD-of-record mode). Effective 2026-06-29.

auditors.clinical: FAIL→FIXED (2026-06-29) — caught a relaxed locked gate: FAM06 dropped the pituitary-tumor screen (kisspeptin-10/gonadorelin GnRH-axis) and narrowed hormone-sensitive malignancy to breast/prostate. FIXED: restored full breast/prostate/endometrial/ovarian set + added the pituitary-tumor/adenoma hard stop; repointed FAM06 delegated ref to the authoritative locked key_gate. Remainder clinically sound (universal hold correctly delegates without relaxing; pro-angiogenic active-OR-prior-malignancy + GHK-Cu Wilson stops correct; GLP pancreatitis/gallbladder/hypoglycemia map to GLP_GATE_002/003).
auditors.citation_regulatory: FAIL→FIXED (2026-06-29) — APPLIED: added meta.governance_posture mirroring the locked iv-module (advisory-by-default; MD-of-record exception; 'hard stop' = clinical advisory not enforcement); replaced verbatim vital thresholds with delegated category labels (no numeric drift); corrected Niagen wording (reference, not 'locked'); added claim_discipline_ref. No efficacy/synergy overclaim present; AOD-9604 efficacy-limitation accurate.
auditors.schema: PASS-WITH-NOTES→FIXED (2026-06-29) — strict-valid JSON; all 9 family_ids + gate_package_strings match peptide-stack-families.json exactly; all ING_*/GLP_GATE_*/IVIM_GATE_VITALS/AXIS_* references resolve. The one defect (dangling peptide-stack-family-safety-gate-map ref for FAM04–09) FIXED by promoting protocols/peptide-stack-family-safety-gate-map.json so the references resolve. Renderer must null-guard optional fields (niagen_note, claim_discipline, review_not_stop). Tracked item RESOLVED 2026-06-29: ING_NIAGEN_NR is now a locked iv-module ingredient (+ protocol WEL-16) in v1.1.
